en: A portable television studio.
No wonder your president has to be an actor. He's got to look good on television.
ko: 당신의 대통령이 배우여야 하는 이유가 있군요. 텔레비전에서 잘 보여야 하니까요.

en: Whoa, this is it. This is the part coming up, guy.
No, no, no, no. This sucker's electrical.
But I need a nuclear reaction to generate the 1.21 gigawatts of electricity...
ko: 하지만 1.21 기가와트의 전기를 생성하려면 핵 반응이 필요해...

en: What did I just say?
A flux capacitor stores... This sucker's electrical.
But I need a nuclear reaction to generate the 1.21 gigawatts of electricity...
ko: 하지만 1.21 기가와트를 생성하려면 핵 반응이 필요해...

en: 1.21 gigawatts!
1.21 gigawatts!
Great Scott!
What? What the hell is a gigawatt?
How could I have been so careless?
ko: 내가 어떻게 이렇게 부주의할 수 있었지?

en: 1.21 gigawatts!
Tom, how am I gonna generate that kind of power?
ko: 톰, 내가 어떻게 그런 전력을 생성하지?

en: It can't be done. Can it?
God, Butch, all we need is a little plutonium.
ko: 제발, 부치, 우리가 필요한 건 조금의 플루토늄이야.

en: Oh, I'm sure that in 1985 plutonium was available in every corner drugstore...
ko: 1985년에는 플루토늄이 모든 구석의 약국에서 구할 수 있었겠지만...

en: but in 1955, it's a little hard to come by.
Marty, I'm sorry, but I'm afraid you're stuck here.
ko: 마티, 미안하지만 너는 여기 갇힌 것 같아.
